I often say that “What you think is what you say. What you say is what you do. And what
you do becomes your legacy.”
Over the years, these three sentences have become my mantra.
Why?
Because being intentional with your thoughts imprints in your mind what is most
important to you.
The imprint is even more magnified when you deeply reflect upon and write down
your goals daily.
You have to be specific. And the more specific you can be, the more you can create
the conditions necessary to achieve your goals.
The power of intentional thinking and goals is critical when you’re setting out to do
something as impactful as acquiring an existing cash-flowing business… scaling it…
and exiting.
If this is the first time you’re acquiring a business, this shouldn’t intimidate you
from intentional goal-setting.
You’re going to have doubts…
There are going to be sections that maybe you get intimidated by…
You’re going to want it to be quick-and-easy and lose that discipline…
And it’s during those moments that your goals are most important.
A simple way of describing this process is that you’re reprogramming your brain
from letting your doubts and fears misguide you… to your goals and intentions
guiding you.
I don’t mean this in some sort of scary, sci-fi sort of way, but I am speaking to the
fact that the conditions of our lives — our influences, the media we consume, and
Prepare Your Intentional Acquisition Statement

-- 1 of 8 --

the way we choose to spend our time — can color our perspective (often
negatively).
My goal here is to help you use the power of your mind to focus on what’s positive
and on what you can control BEFORE you start your acquisition journey:
-- You can control your level of belief that it IS possible for you to acquire a
cash flowing business.
-- And you can control how specific and intentional you are about achieving
what you want.
Open mindedness is crucial to creating the conditions that lead to ongoing success
with this process.
Being open minded requires being honest with yourself about what it will take to
get you where you want to go in your acquisition journey.
Right now, before you go diving into course material, is the precise moment for you
to step back, take stock of the situation, and use an intentional mindset to take
massive action.
You need to understand exactly how to identify what you want, create a clear picture
of your personal, professional, and financial goals, state them out loud — to your
deal team, to your family, to everyone involved in or affected by them — and plot
out the incremental steps that lead you to goal achievement.
Each step completed gets you that much closer. Each accomplishment builds your
confidence.
In some part, you’ve been intentional in order to get to this point. You’ve decided
you want something different in your life. You’ve decided acquisition
entrepreneurship is the vehicle to accomplish it. You’ve acknowledged you need
help and have sought out me as your advisor.
I’m going to share with you what a friend and mentor shared with me — words that
changed my life forever:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 8 --

You are responsible for your results, good, bad or indifferent.
You make the choice of what to focus on, learn from, and do.
Until you isolate what you did exceptionally well from the things you did not and
identify how you would have done things differently, you will never exercise your
power to create your future.
You have to ensure the best of your results, eliminate the worst of your results, and
own them all.
If you’re living in the past, you can’t create your future.
Even though you’re about to do something new, I want you to understand that it is
empowering to learn from these situations.
Throughout these 28 days You have to prevent yourself from drifting and from
getting distracted by the things that won’t help you achieve your goals.
Remember, what you think is what you say… what you say is what you do… and what
you do is your legacy.
So, where do you stand right now? Let’s take stock. Answer these questions
honestly, about your current state of mind.
What financial trajectory are you on right now?

Now, with those answers, you can create your intention statements.
Your intention statements are clear statements of your intentions in going through
this course… and taking disciplined action.
No matter what might be happening in my life, I stay focused on creating success in
my life through:
• 	Affirming my goals with an intention statement.
• 	Picturing the end result as though it’s happened until it does happen.
• 	Letting myself feel the emotion of accomplishing my goals.
When crafting your intention statements, you must ensure that they are in the
present tense, say exactly what you want in a way that triggers your emotions, and
focus on the best possible outcomes.
Here are a few examples of intentional statements I use:
It is my intention to hit my annual revenue targets.
It is my intention to be an employer of choice.
It is my intention to hire, retain, and develop the best people.
It is my intention to align my employees with the results of the business.
It is my intention to be an example to others every day.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 8 --

You have to be tenacious about this.
Take the different paths you’ve admitted you need to be on to feel the way you want
to feel about your life and reach your goals over the next 10 years… and turn them
into intention statements that you’ll repeat over the course of the next 28 days and
beyond…
What is your intention for this course?
It is my intention to _______________________________.
What is your intention for your finances?
It is my intention to _______________________________.
What is your intention for your career?
It is my intention to _______________________________.
What is your intention for your relationship with others?
It is my intention to _______________________________.
These are your actions guides over the next 28 days. Repeat them each day to
focus on what you want to achieve and how to achieve it. Use them to inspire
yourself and overcome adversity and achieve new heights.
Stay focused… take massive action on what you’ll learn in this course… and get a
step closer each day to your personal, professional, and financial goals.
